entries.change_body: Fixed `Stripe` constructor config parameter to use `StripeConfig` type instead of `Record<string, unknown>`, restoring compile-time type safety.

entries.change_body: Fixed `Stripe.API_VERSION` to retain the literal API version type.

entries.change_body: Fixed `StripeConfig.stripeContext` to accept `StripeContext` objects in addition to strings.

entries.change_title: [#2619](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2619) Improved TypeScript support in the Node SDK
entries.change_body: [#2619](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2619) Improved TypeScript support in the Node SDK

entries.change_title: Moved the types from the partially manually maintained and partially generated types folder to be inline with the imp...
entries.change_body: Moved the types from the partially manually maintained and partially generated types folder to be inline with the implementation in TypeScript files.

entries.change_title: Removed top-level “stripe” ambient module.
entries.change_body: Removed top-level “stripe” ambient module. This allows import aliasing for the stripe package.

entries.change_title: ⚠️ `Stripe.StripeContext` is no longer exported as a type.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ `Stripe.StripeContext` is no longer exported as a type. Use `Stripe.StripeContextType` instead.

entries.change_title: ⚠️ `Stripe.errors.StripeError` is no longer a type.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ `Stripe.errors.StripeError` is no longer a type. Use `typeof Stripe.errors.StripeError` or `Stripe.ErrorType` instead.

entries.change_title: ⚠️ CJS entry point no longer exports .default or .Stripe as separate properties.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ CJS entry point no longer exports .default or .Stripe as separate properties.

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Stripe import is now a true ES6 class.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Stripe import is now a true ES6 class. Use `new Stripe()` to create a StripeClient instead of calling it:

entries.change_title: [#2645](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2645) ⚠️ Remove `stripeMethod` and standardize how function args a...
entries.change_body: [#2645](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2645) ⚠️ Remove `stripeMethod` and standardize how function args are handled (including removing callback support)

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Refactor how incoming method arguments are parsed.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Refactor how incoming method arguments are parsed. Type signatures for API methods should be _much_ more accurate and reliable now

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Remove support for providing callbacks to API methods.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Remove support for providing callbacks to API methods. Use `async / await` instead

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Remove support for passing a plain API key as a function arg.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Remove support for passing a plain API key as a function arg. If supplied on a per-request basis, it should be in the `RequestOptions` under the `apiKey` property

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Keys from `params` and `options` objects are no longer mixed.
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Keys from `params` and `options` objects are no longer mixed. If present on a method, `RequestParams` must always come first and `RequestOptions` must always come second. To supply options without params, pass `undefined` as the first argument explicitly

entries.change_title: ⚠️ Removed methods from `StripeResource`: `createFullPath`, `createResourcePathWithSymbols`, `extend`, `method` and `...
entries.change_body: ⚠️ Removed methods from `StripeResource`: `createFullPath`, `createResourcePathWithSymbols`, `extend`, `method` and `_joinUrlParts`. These were mostly intended for internal use and we no longer need them

entries.change_title: [#2643](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2643) ⚠️ Removed per-request host override.
entries.change_body: [#2643](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/pull/2643) ⚠️ Removed per-request host override. To use a custom host, set it in the client configuration. All requests from that client will use that host.

entries.change_title: All `decimal_string` fields changed type from `string` to `Stripe.Decimal` in both request params and response objects.
entries.change_body: All `decimal_string` fields changed type from `string` to `Stripe.Decimal` in both request params and response objects. Code that reads or writes these fields as `string` will need to use `Stripe.Decimal` instead (construct via `Decimal.from("1.23")`, serialize via `.toString()`). Affected fields across v1 and v2 APIs:
